conservation_status: Mixed — several Alberta bumble bees are at risk
source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Alberta has ~28 bumble bee species; among the richest bumble bee habitat in North America.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Mining bees — solitary ground nesters and key early-spring pollinators; many are pollen specialists.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Mason bees — cavity nesters that seal cells with mud; readily use bee hotels and drilled logs.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Leafcutter bees — cavity nesters that line cells with cut leaf discs; use bee hotels.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Sweat bees — ground nesters, often in bare compact soil; long adult season.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Small sweat bees — abundant ground nesters across the whole growing season.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Long-horned bees — late-summer ground nesters strongly tied to Asteraceae (sunflowers, asters, coneflowers).

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Blue orchard mason bee — early-spring cavity nester; an efficient fruit-tree pollinator.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: A leafcutter bee — mid-summer cavity nester.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Bicoloured sweat bee — communal ground nester with a metallic-green head.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Spring cellophane bee — early ground nester that lines cells with a waterproof film; key willow pollinator.

source: Alberta native bee compilation (Alberta Native Bee Council); nesting & floral hosts after Sheffield, Frier & Dumesh 2014 (Bees of Canadian Grasslands) and Wilson & Carril 2015; genus spellings normalised; flight windows generalised for Alberta (P9 — coarse ranges).
notes: Orange-legged furrow bee — a common, sometimes-social ground-nesting sweat bee.
